title: Who wants to be a ‘Millionaire’? Berkshires town keeps its divisive mascot following vote
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Education A recent poll on the unusual mascot found no “clear or decisive consensus” within Lenox’s school community.
Shutterstock ShareSend this article to your social connections.
Lenox will keep its controversial “Millionaires” mascot after students and families came out divided on whether to buck the ritzy moniker or embrace their Berkshires town’s Gilded Age tradition.

title: The Celtics are on the brink after 76ers take Game 6: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Boston Celtics Jayson Tatum left Thursday's game and did not return, though he says he isn't overly concerned.

Philadelphia 76ers' Paul George, left, chases a loose ball past the Boston Celtics' Neemias Queta during the second half of Game 6 in a first-round NBA basketball playoff series Thursday, April 30, 2026, in Philadelphia.

title: South End candy shop reports being robbed at knifepoint
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Crime “At the end of the day, we are not going to let this loser man’s actions ruin the vibe of our happy place,” the business said.
Boston Police Department A popular South End Swedish candy store was robbed at knifepoint Tuesday, police and the business said.

“At the end of the day, we are not going to let this loser man’s actions ruin the vibe of our happy place,” Madeleine’s Candy Shop, located on Clarendon Street in the South End, said in a social media post.

title: Boston Magazine names Drake Maye, Mike Vrabel as ‘Most Influential Bostonians’ for 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: New England Patriots “Yes, they lost the Super Bowl.
And it stung like the bitter cold.
But they showed this city what it looks like to overcome adversity — on the field and off.

title: Person wearing KKK robes in R.I. was pulling a social media stunt, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Local News A West Warwick resident captured video of the white-robed figure wandering around town early Monday morning.
Police in Rhode Island say they’ve confirmed that videos of a person walking around a town in a Ku Klux Klan robe and hood were part of a hoax for social media, not the work of an organized group.
Social media videos appeared to show the person walking around West Warwick while dressed in white robes and a pointed hood.
